Patch Set 11: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I don't think putting extra-argument everywhere is the right solution here.

AFAIU, this can't go into 2.4 and 2.5 is not open yet, so we do have some time to think of this in the context of the changes planned for 2.5 (e.g. turning MDD into a specific library and letting MDT to be involved into transactions directly).

Patch Set 14: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 14: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

(1 inline comment)

Patch Set 14: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(13 inline comments)

It appears that this patch does not address interoperability issues with earlier versions of Lustre that I can see.

1) What happens if a new client connects to an old MDS?
2) What happens if an old client connects to a new MDS?
3) What happens if an MDT is upgraded with ChangeLog entries that have mixed record sizes?
4) What happens with old binaries on a new client?

I think problem (1) and (3) are addressed by CLF_HAS_JOBID, but (2) and (4) appear to be missing.  Ideally, if the application doesn't open the changelog with a new CHANGELOG_FLAG_JOBID then either the kernel or library would filter out the cr_jobid field from the changelog record and convert it to the old changelog_rec structure.

Patch Set 14: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

seems the patch needs some updates..

Patch Set 14:

I'm currently working on the changes that Andreas suggested, i.e. having struct changelog_rec_v2 (and its extended sibling), to ensure full compatibility between new and old applications, clients and servers.

Patch Set 16:

Alex, the current structure has a variable-length filename at the end, so it makes it inconvenient to get the jobid from the structure.

Patch Set 16:

yes, I noticed name, but I'd think it's pretty easy to get an offset? not sure... I mean I'm fine if you think this approach is better, it's just that the amount of code we get due to another structure is much more than a few lines macro?

Patch Set 16:

Since there are only a very small number of users of this struct, I hope we can just drop support for the old format soon, if this gets into 2.5. Otherwise we need to keep it around longer. As you often write - we don't want to design the interface around compatibility, but think about how it is best.
Patch Set 16: Looks good to me, approved

Patch Set 16: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Andreas, in the other bug we were discussing compatibility issues and it was said there is no reason to restrict users to upgrade over versions. so it's not clear when we'll be able to drop support for v1 changelog format. this new format also means we'll be writing 32 more bytes every time, even when there is no need in jobid tracking. not sure whether this is important or not.

Patch Set 18: Code-Review-1

(27 comments)

Sorry, but having looked at this code with the compatibility changes, it seems that it is getting overly complex, and will just continue to get worse (both in space wasted in the records and in code complexity) as more fields/flags are added to the changelog records.

I propose inline a better way to handle these records in an extensible manner that does not push complexity out to every piece of code that needs to access the changelog_rec, but rather keeps it in a single place in the code.

Patch Set 18:

Thanks a lot Andreas. I'll soon propose another implementation following your advices.

parents.subject: LU-5624 tests: ignore bad lfsck performance for ZFS backend

commit.subject: LU-1996 lustre: Flexible changelog format.
commit.message:
LU-1996 lustre: Flexible changelog format.

Added jobid fields to Changelog records (and extended records). The
CLF_JOBID flags allows to check if the field is present or not (old
format) when reading an entry. Jobids are expressed as 32 chars long,
zero-terminated strings. Updated test_205 in sanity.sh.

Signed-off-by: Henri Doreau <henri.doreau@cea.fr>
Change-Id: Iacfe7099aa520e012535ac48c44793a1b86870a9
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/4060
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Aurelien Degremont <aurelien.degremont@cea.fr>
